title: DualProcessTheoryModel
description: Defines the agent's cognitive routing: defaulting to speed/efficiency unless deep engagement is triggered by goal alignment or interest.

default_pathway.description: The System 1 (Fast) pathway. Optimized purely for speed, latency, and minimal resource usage.

engagement_triggers.description: The conditions that justify spending time/resources to switch to the deep pathway.

goal_alignment_threshold.description: How closely the task must align with short/long-term goals to trigger deep thinking.

curiosity_override.description: If true, high novelty/interest tasks bypass the goal alignment check.

critical_failure_risk.description: If the perceived risk of failure exceeds this, it forces deep thinking.

deep_pathway.description: The System 2 (Slow) pathway. Triggered when genuine interest or goal alignment is present.
